instruction:
Let $f \left(x \right)$be a continuous function and $f \left(x \right)=\operatorname{c o s} x-\int_{0}^{x} \left(x-t \right) f \left(t \right) \mathrm{d} t$, then $f \left(x \right)$=().

Options:
A. $$ \operatorname2{c o s} x+\frac{1} {2} x \operatorname{s i n} x $$
B. $$ \operatorname{c o s} x+\frac{1} {3} x \operatorname{s i n} x $$
C. $$ \operatorname{c o s} x+\frac{3} {4} x \operatorname{s i n} x $$
D. $$ \operatorname{c o s} x+\frac{2} {3} x \operatorname{s i n} x $$
E. $$ \operatorname3{c o s} x+\frac{1} {2} x \operatorname{s i n} x $$
F. $$ \operatorname{c o s} x+\frac{1} {2} x \operatorname{s i n} 2x $$
G. $$ \operatorname{c o s} 2x+\frac{1} {2} x \operatorname{s i n} x $$
H. $$ \operatorname{c o s} x+\frac{1} {2} x \operatorname{s i n} 3x $$
I. $$\operatorname{c o s} x-\frac{1} {2} x \operatorname{s i n} x $$
